human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for returns-and-exchange boundary discipline, source quality, payment and serial evidence handling, safety and fraud escalation posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to electronics and appliance return or exchange execution, order and tender matching, serial verification, inspection review, inventory disposition, and escalation packaging.
Authority sources cover FTC order and warranty guidance, California refund-policy law, CFPB billing-dispute guidance, CPSC recall handling, and PHMSA reverse-logistics constraints.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, missing completion evidence, order and payment mismatch, policy exceptions, recall and damaged battery safety stops, high-volume intake, fraud signals, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, and conflicting-policy handling.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-electronics-and-appliance-retail-and-service/returns-and-exchange-specialist/ so returns-and-exchange execution remains separate from pricing, guest recovery, fraud, service, and warranty lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around pricing overrides, goodwill refunds, fraud signals, warranty exceptions, and safety-sensitive returns.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated consumer-electronics-and-appliance returns and exchange specialist that supports evidence-backed execution and policy-aware escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
